description: An introduction to the objectives, philosophy, concepts, methods and social implications of environmental impact assessment (EIA). A critical examination of EIA as an analytical tool in the context of resource management and public policy is undertaken. Examples are drawn from B.C., Canada and other countries.
preAndCorequisites:
• Complete all of:
    • GEOG209 - Environmental Management (1.5)
supplementalNotes: • Credit will be granted for only one of GEOG 301, GEOG 391 (if taken in the same topic), ES 400A (if taken prior to September 1989), ES 410, ES 461.
title: Environmental Impact Assessment
